description: Acne, also known as acne vulgaris, is a long-term skin condition that occurs when dead skin cells and oil from the skin clog hair follicles. Typical features include blackheads, whiteheads, pimples, oily skin, and possible scarring. It primarily affects the face, upper chest, and back and may impact psychological well-being including anxiety or reduced self-esteem.
precaution 1: Wash face twice daily with mild cleanser

description: Asthma is a chronic inflammatory disease of the airways characterized by recurrent wheezing, coughing, chest tightness, and shortness of breath. Symptoms may worsen with allergens, dust, exercise, or at night.
Anemia is a blood disorder in which the blood has a reduced ability to carry oxygen due to low red blood cells, low hemoglobin, or abnormal hemoglobin.
Appendicitis is inflammation of the appendix. Symptoms commonly include right lower abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, fever, and decreased appetite. Severe complications include rupture and sepsis.

Anxiety is an emotion characterized by inner turmoil, excessive worry, nervous behavior, and physical symptoms such as palpitations or restlessness. It differs from fear as it anticipates future threats.

Arthritis is a disorder where the smooth cartilage lining a joint is lost, causing bone friction during movement. Symptoms include joint pain, stiffness, redness, swelling, and decreased range of motion.
An allergy is an exaggerated immune response to harmless substances, causing symptoms such as sneezing, rash, coughing, red eyes, shortness of breath, or swelling.
Athlete's foot (tinea pedis) is a fungal infection of the feet causing itching, scaling, cracking, and redness, commonly between the toes.
An anal fissure is a tear in the skin of the anal canal causing bright red bleeding and pain after defecation.
Atrial fibrillation is an abnormal heart rhythm characterized by rapid and irregular beating of the atria. It may cause palpitations, shortness of breath, and increased stroke risk.
Alzheimer's disease is a neurodegenerative disorder and the most common cause of dementia, characterized by progressive memory loss, language problems, mood changes, and decline in daily functioning.

Balanitis is inflammation of the glans penis. Balanoposthitis is the proper term when the foreskin is also affected. Balanitis in boys in diapers must be distinguished from redness caused by ammoniacal dermatitis.

Bell's palsy is a type of facial paralysis that results in a temporary inability to control the facial muscles on the affected side of the face. Symptoms may include facial weakness, drooping of the eyebrow, change in taste, ear pain, and hyperacusis. Most cases improve over weeks.

Bipolar disorder is a mental disorder characterized by periods of depression and abnormally elevated mood (mania or hypomania), with possible impulsivity and sleep disturbance. It carries significant suicide risk.
Benign prostatic hyperplasia is a noncancerous enlargement of the prostate gland causing urinary frequency, weak stream, difficulty starting urination, and possible urinary retention.
Benign paroxysmal positional vertigo is an inner ear disorder causing brief episodes of spinning sensation triggered by head movement, often lasting less than one minute and associated with nausea.
Blepharitis is inflammation of the eyelids characterized by redness, scaling, crusting, itching, and burning sensation. It may result from bacterial infection or meibomian gland dysfunction.
Bronchitis is inflammation of the bronchi causing cough, sputum production, wheezing, shortness of breath, and chest discomfort. It may be acute or chronic.
A burn is an injury to skin or tissues caused by heat, electricity, chemicals, friction, or radiation. Severity ranges from mild redness to deep tissue damage.
precaution 3: Seek medical care for deep burns

description: Bursitis is inflammation of bursae causing pain, swelling, warmth, and reduced movement, commonly affecting shoulder, elbow, hip, or knee.
A breast cyst is a fluid-filled sac within the breast, often described as a soft or firm lump with distinct edges.
Cardiomyopathy is a group of diseases of the heart muscle that may cause shortness of breath, fatigue, leg swelling, arrhythmias, or sudden cardiac death.
Carpal tunnel syndrome is a nerve compression syndrome caused by compression of the median nerve at the wrist, leading to numbness, tingling, and weakness in the hand.
A cataract is a cloudy area in the lens of the eye leading to decreased vision, blurred sight, glare sensitivity, and difficulty seeing at night.
Celiac disease is a chronic autoimmune disease of the small intestine caused by immune reaction to gluten in wheat, barley, and rye.
An abscess is a collection of pus within tissue caused by bacterial infection, presenting with redness, pain, warmth, and swelling.
Cervical spondylosis is age-related degeneration of the cervical spine that may cause neck pain, stiffness, paresthesia, imbalance, or limb weakness.
Cervicitis is inflammation of the uterine cervix, often caused by infection or irritation.
Chickenpox is a highly contagious viral disease causing itchy blisters, fever, and fatigue.
precaution 3: Keep nails trimmed to prevent scarring

description: Chikungunya is a viral infection transmitted by mosquitoes, causing fever and severe joint pain.
Cholecystitis is inflammation of the gallbladder causing right upper abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, and sometimes fever.
Cholera is an infection of the small intestine causing severe watery diarrhea leading to dehydration.
Chronic back pain is pain in the back lasting more than three months, often characterized as dull, shooting, or burning.
Chronic kidney disease is long-term abnormal kidney function lasting at least three months and may lead to dialysis or transplantation.
Cirrhosis is chronic liver disease where normal liver tissue is replaced by scar tissue, impairing liver function and causing portal hypertension.
The common cold is a viral infection of the upper respiratory tract causing cough, runny nose, sore throat, and mild fever.
Conjunctivitis is inflammation of the conjunctiva causing redness, itching, discharge, and tearing.
Constipation is infrequent or difficult bowel movement with hard stool and abdominal discomfort.
Contact dermatitis is inflammation of the skin caused by exposure to irritants or allergens, presenting with rash, itching, and redness.
Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) is a progressive lung disease causing chronic cough, sputum production, and airflow limitation.
Corneal abrasion is a scratch on the surface of the cornea causing pain, redness, and light sensitivity.
Crohn's disease is an inflammatory bowel disease affecting any part of the gastrointestinal tract causing abdominal pain, diarrhea, and weight loss.
Croup is a viral respiratory infection causing a barking cough, stridor, and hoarseness, usually in young children.

De Quervain syndrome or De Quervain tendinopathy occurs when two tendons that control movement of the thumb become enlarged where they pass through their tendon sheath in the wrist. The characteristic symptoms are pain and tenderness on the thumb side of the wrist. Radial abduction of the thumb is painful. On some occasions, there is uneven movement or triggering of the thumb and wrist. Symptoms can develop gradually or suddenly.
Deafness in medical context refers to hearing loss severe enough to impair understanding of spoken language. It may be congenital or acquired and varies in severity.
precaution 3: Use hearing aids/cochlear implants when indicated

description: Pressure ulcers, also known as pressure sores or bed sores, are localized damage to the skin and underlying tissue usually over bony prominences due to prolonged pressure, shear, or friction. Common sites include sacrum, heels, hips, elbows, and occiput.

Dementia is a progressive neurocognitive disorder characterized by decline in memory, thinking, behavior, and ability to perform daily activities. It is distinct from normal age-related cognitive decline.
Dengue fever is a mosquito-borne viral disease common in tropical areas, presenting with high fever, headache, vomiting, muscle and joint pain, and rash. Severe cases may lead to bleeding and shock.
Tooth decay, also known as caries, is the breakdown of teeth due to acids produced by bacteria, leading to cavities, pain, and possible abscess formation.
precaution 1: Brush twice daily with fluoride toothpaste

description: Major depressive disorder is a mental disorder characterized by at least two weeks of pervasive low mood, low self-esteem, and loss of interest in pleasurable activities, causing significant functional impairment.
Nasal septum deviation is displacement of the nasal septum, which may cause nasal obstruction, difficulty breathing, snoring, or recurrent sinus infections.

Diabetes mellitus is a group of endocrine diseases characterized by sustained high blood sugar levels due to insulin deficiency or resistance, leading to long-term complications affecting eyes, kidneys, nerves, and heart.
Diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) is a life-threatening acute complication of diabetes characterized by vomiting, abdominal pain, deep breathing, fruity breath odor, confusion, and possible coma.
Diabetic peripheral neuropathy is nerve damage associated with diabetes, causing numbness, tingling, pain, or weakness, commonly in the feet and hands.
Diverticulitis is inflammation of diverticula in the colon causing sudden lower abdominal pain, fever, nausea, and bowel habit changes.
Dry eye disease is a condition of insufficient tear production or excessive tear evaporation causing irritation, burning, redness, and foreign body sensation.
Dysentery is a form of gastroenteritis causing bloody diarrhea, abdominal pain, and fever, potentially leading to dehydration.

An eating disorder is a mental disorder defined by abnormal eating behaviors that adversely affect a person's physical or mental health. Types include binge eating disorder, anorexia nervosa, bulimia nervosa, pica, rumination syndrome, and ARFID. Anxiety, depression, and substance abuse are common comorbidities.

Dermatitis is a term used for different types of skin inflammation, typically characterized by itchiness, redness, and rash. In short duration there may be blisters; in long-term cases the skin may become thickened. Eczema commonly refers to atopic dermatitis.
Endometriosis is a disease in which tissue similar to the lining of the uterus grows outside the uterus, commonly on ovaries or fallopian tubes, causing pelvic pain and infertility.
Epididymitis is inflammation of the epididymis causing gradual onset scrotal pain, swelling, burning urination, and possible fever.
Epilepsy is a neurological disorder characterized by recurrent, unprovoked seizures due to abnormal electrical activity in the brain.
Erectile dysfunction is a condition characterized by the persistent inability to achieve or maintain an erection sufficient for satisfactory sexual activity.
Esophagitis is inflammation of the esophagus, often caused by acid reflux, infection, or medication injury.
precaution 3: Use proton pump inhibitors if prescribed

description: Strabismus is an eye disorder in which the eyes do not properly align when looking at an object. It may be constant or intermittent and can lead to amblyopia in children or double vision in adults.
is the formation of a blood clot in a deep vein, commonly in the legs, which may cause swelling, pain, and risk of pulmonary embolism.
Fibroadenomas are benign breast tumours characterized by an admixture of stromal and epithelial tissue developing from the lobules, forming a solid, mobile lump in the breast.

Fibrocystic breast changes is a condition where there may be breast pain, cysts, and lumpy breast tissue, often worsening during parts of the menstrual cycle due to hormonal stimulation. These are normal breast changes and not associated with cancer.

Fibromyalgia is a long-term health condition characterized by widespread chronic pain along with fatigue, cognitive disturbance, unrefreshing sleep, abdominal cramps, depression, or headache. The exact cause remains unclear.

Influenza is an infectious disease caused by influenza viruses. Symptoms include fever, sore throat, runny nose, muscle pain, headache, cough, and fatigue. Symptoms begin one to four days after exposure and last two to eight days. Complications may include pneumonia or worsening of pre-existing conditions.
precaution 3: Early antiviral therapy if high risk

description: Folate deficiency is a low level of vitamin B9 resulting in megaloblastic anemia. Symptoms may include fatigue, palpitations, shortness of breath, irritability, and during pregnancy may increase risk of neural tube defects.
A food allergy is an abnormal immune response to food, causing symptoms such as itching, swelling, vomiting, hives, breathing difficulty, or low blood pressure. Severe cases result in anaphylaxis.
Fungal infection (mycosis) is a disease caused by fungi. Superficial infections affect skin, presenting as red, itchy, scaly rashes such as tinea (ringworm).
Dermatophytosis of the hair is a fungal infection affecting the scalp and hair shafts, causing itchy, scaly patches and hair loss.
Frostbite is injury to skin or tissue caused by freezing, commonly affecting fingers, toes, ears, nose, and cheeks. Skin may appear pale, numb, or waxy, with possible blistering after rewarming.

A gallstone is a stone formed within the gallbladder from precipitated bile components. Cholelithiasis refers to the presence of gallstones; choledocholithiasis refers to migrated gallstones within bile ducts.
Gastroenteritis is inflammation of the stomach and intestine, commonly presenting with diarrhea, vomiting, abdominal pain, fever, and dehydration. It usually lasts less than two weeks.
Gastroparesis is a disorder of ineffective stomach contractions resulting in delayed gastric emptying and prolonged retention of food.
Genital herpes is a herpes simplex virus infection of the genitals. When symptomatic, it includes painful blisters that form ulcers and may be accompanied by flu-like symptoms.
Gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) is a chronic condition where stomach contents flow back into the esophagus, causing heartburn, regurgitation, chest discomfort, cough, or hoarseness.
Gestational diabetes is high blood sugar that develops during pregnancy in individuals without prior diabetes. It may increase risk of macrosomia, neonatal hypoglycemia, and future type 2 diabetes.
Glaucoma is a group of eye diseases that damage the optic nerve, often due to increased intraocular pressure, and may cause gradual vision loss if untreated.
A goiter is swelling in the neck due to enlargement of the thyroid gland and may be associated with normal, increased, or decreased thyroid function.
Gonorrhea is a sexually transmitted infection caused by Neisseria gonorrhoeae that can infect the genitals, rectum, or throat.
Gout is an inflammatory arthritis characterized by sudden, severe pain in a red, hot, swollen joint due to uric acid crystal deposition, often affecting the big toe.
Graves' disease is an autoimmune disease affecting the thyroid, commonly causing hyperthyroidism with symptoms such as weight loss, heat intolerance, rapid heartbeat, and eye bulging.
Gynecomastia is benign enlargement of male breast tissue due to hormonal imbalance between estrogens and androgens.
Hashimoto's thyroiditis, also known as chronic lymphocytic thyroiditis, Hashimoto's disease and autoimmune thyroiditis, is an autoimmune disease in which the thyroid gland is gradually destroyed.

Allergic rhinitis, of which the seasonal type is called hay fever, is a type of inflammation in the nose that occurs when the immune system overreacts to allergens in the air. Signs and symptoms include runny nose, sneezing, red, itchy watery eyes, and swelling around the eyes.
Heart failure (HF), also known as congestive heart failure (CHF), is a syndrome caused by impairment in the heart's ability to fill with and pump blood.
Heat exhaustion is a heat-related illness characterized by inability to effectively cool the body, with symptoms such as sweating, dizziness, weakness, nausea, and dehydration.

Hemorrhoids, also known as piles, are vascular structures in the anal canal that become swollen or inflamed. Internal hemorrhoids cause painless rectal bleeding; external hemorrhoids cause pain and swelling.
Hepatitis A is an infectious liver disease caused by Hepatitis A virus (HAV). Symptoms may include nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, jaundice, fever, and abdominal pain.
Hepatitis B is an infectious disease caused by the hepatitis B virus (HBV) that affects the liver; it can cause acute or chronic infection.
Hepatitis C is an infectious disease caused by the hepatitis C virus (HCV) that primarily affects the liver and may become chronic.
Shingles, also known as herpes zoster, is a viral disease characterized by a painful localized rash with blisters, sometimes followed by postherpetic neuralgia.
A hiatal hernia is a condition where abdominal organs slip through the diaphragm into the chest, often causing reflux symptoms.
Hidradenitis suppurativa is a chronic dermatological condition characterized by painful inflamed lumps in areas such as underarms and groin, often leading to scarring.
Hypertension is a long-term medical condition in which blood pressure in the arteries is persistently elevated and increases risk of stroke, heart disease, kidney disease, and dementia.
Hyperlipidemia is abnormally high levels of lipids or lipoproteins in the blood and is usually chronic.
Hyperhidrosis is a condition characterized by excessive sweating beyond what is required for body temperature regulation.
Hypoglycemia is a fall in blood sugar below normal, typically below 70 mg/dL, causing symptoms such as shakiness, confusion, sweating, or seizures.

Dysmenorrhea, also known as period pain, painful periods or menstrual cramps, is pain during menstruation. Its usual onset occurs around the time that menstruation begins. Symptoms typically last less than three days. The pain is usually in the pelvis or lower abdomen. Other symptoms may include back pain, diarrhea or nausea.
Abnormal uterine bleeding is vaginal bleeding from the uterus that is abnormally frequent, lasts excessively long, is heavier than normal, or is irregular. Quality of life may be negatively affected.

Impetigo is a contagious bacterial infection that involves the superficial skin. The most common presentation is yellowish crusts on the face, arms, or legs. Less commonly there may be large blisters which affect the groin or armpits.
Irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) is a functional gastrointestinal disorder characterized by abdominal pain, bloating, and altered bowel habits.
Jaundice is a yellowish pigmentation of the skin and sclera due to high bilirubin levels. Associated symptoms may include itchiness, pale feces, and dark urine.
Juvenile idiopathic arthritis is the most common chronic rheumatic disease of childhood, referring to arthritis before 16 years of age.

Kidney stone disease or urinary stone disease is a crystallopathy that occurs when there is an excess of minerals in the urine and not enough liquid or hydration. This imbalance causes tiny pieces of crystal to aggregate and form hard masses (stones) in the urinary tract. Stones larger than 5 mm may cause severe lower back pain radiating to the groin, blood in urine, vomiting, or painful urination.

Lactose intolerance is caused by a lessened ability or complete inability to digest lactose, a sugar found in dairy products. Symptoms include abdominal pain, bloating, diarrhea, flatulence, and nausea after consuming lactose-containing foods. It does not cause damage to the gastrointestinal tract.

Laryngitis is inflammation of the larynx. Symptoms often include a hoarse voice to no voice at all and may include fever, cough, pain in the front of the neck, and trouble swallowing. Typically these last under 2 weeks.

Lichen planus (LP) is a chronic inflammatory and autoimmune disease affecting skin, nails, hair, and mucous membranes. It is characterized by flat-topped, violaceous papules and plaques. The cause is unknown but thought to be autoimmune.

Lyme disease is a tick-borne disease caused by Borrelia bacteria. Symptoms may include fever, fatigue, headache, and a characteristic skin rash. Early detection and prompt antibiotic treatment lead to favorable outcomes.

Macular degeneration is a medical condition which may result in blurred or no vision in the center of the visual field. Early on there are often no symptoms; later gradual worsening of vision may occur.

Malaria is a mosquito-borne infectious disease transmitted by Anopheles mosquitoes. Symptoms include fever, fatigue, vomiting, and headaches. Severe cases can cause jaundice, seizures, coma, or death.
Mastitis is inflammation of the breast, usually associated with breastfeeding. Symptoms include breast pain, redness, fever, and general soreness.
Measles is a highly contagious, vaccine-preventable infectious disease caused by measles virus.
Ménière's disease is a disorder of the inner ear characterized by episodes of vertigo, tinnitus, hearing loss, and ear fullness.
Migraine is a neurological disorder characterized by episodes of moderate-to-severe headache, often unilateral and associated with nausea and light sensitivity.
Infectious mononucleosis is an infection usually caused by Epstein–Barr virus (EBV). Symptoms include fever, sore throat, enlarged lymph nodes, and fatigue.

Nasal polyps are noncancerous growths within the nose or sinuses. Symptoms include trouble breathing through the nose, loss of smell, decreased taste, post nasal drip, and a runny nose. The growths are sac-like, movable, and nontender.
Shoulder impingement syndrome involves tendonitis of the rotator cuff muscles as they pass through the subacromial space. It can result in pain, weakness, and loss of movement at the shoulder.
Peripheral neuropathy refers to damage affecting the nerves. Drug-induced neuropathy may impair sensation or movement depending on affected fibers.

Obesity is a medical condition in which excess body fat accumulates to the extent that it negatively affects health. It is defined as BMI over 30 kg/m² and is associated with cardiovascular disease, type 2 diabetes, sleep apnea, and osteoarthritis.
Obsessive–compulsive disorder (OCD) is a mental disorder characterized by intrusive thoughts and repetitive behaviors performed to relieve distress, impairing daily functioning.
Onychomycosis is a fungal infection of the nail causing discoloration, thickening, and separation from the nail bed.
Obstructive sleep apnea (OSA) is a sleep disorder characterized by recurrent episodes of upper airway obstruction during sleep, leading to reduced breathing and oxygen levels.
Osteoarthritis is a degenerative joint disease resulting from breakdown of joint cartilage and underlying bone, causing joint pain and stiffness.
Osteoporosis is a systemic skeletal disorder characterized by low bone mass and increased fracture risk due to bone weakening.
Otitis media is an inflammatory disease of the middle ear, often presenting with ear pain and sometimes fever.
precaution 3: Avoid bottle feeding while lying down (infants)

description: An ovarian cyst is a fluid-filled sac within the ovary. They usually cause no symptoms, but may produce bloating or lower abdominal pain.
precaution 3: Seek care if sudden severe pain

description: Parkinson's disease is a neurodegenerative disease affecting motor and non-motor systems. Motor symptoms include tremor, bradykinesia, rigidity, and balance difficulty. Non-motor symptoms include sleep abnormalities, anosmia, and cognitive impairment.
Paronychia is an inflammation of the skin around the nail, often due to bacteria or fungi.

Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) is the most common hormonal disorder in women of reproductive age. The name comes from the observation of small follicles that often appear on the ovaries, though not all individuals have these follicles.
precaution 3: Monitor menstrual cycle and insulin levels

description: Pelvic inflammatory disease (PID) is an infection of the upper female reproductive system including uterus, fallopian tubes, and ovaries. Symptoms may include lower abdominal pain, discharge, fever, painful urination, or irregular bleeding.

Peptic ulcer disease refers to damage of the gastric mucosa or duodenum. Symptoms include upper abdominal burning pain, sometimes improving or worsening with meals. Complications may include bleeding or perforation.
Plantar fasciitis is inflammation of the plantar fascia causing heel pain, especially with first steps in the morning.
Pneumonia is an inflammatory condition of the lung affecting alveoli. Symptoms include cough, chest pain, fever, and difficulty breathing.
Psoriasis is a chronic autoimmune disease characterized by red, itchy, scaly skin patches.

Post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) develops after experiencing a traumatic event. Symptoms include intrusive memories, avoidance, mood changes, and increased fight-or-flight response lasting more than a month.
Presbycusis is progressive age-related hearing loss, most marked at higher frequencies.

Raynaud syndrome, also known as Raynaud's phenomenon, is a medical condition in which the spasm of small arteries causes episodes of reduced blood flow to end arterioles. Typically the disease affects the fingers, and, less commonly, the toes, though it rarely also affects the nose, ears, nipples, or lips. The episodes classically result in the affected part turning white and then blue. Often, numbness or pain occurs. As blood flow returns, the area turns red and burns. The episodes typically last minutes but can last several hours.
precaution 1: Avoid cold exposure and keep extremities warm

description: Restless legs syndrome (RLS), also known as Willis–Ekbom disease (WED), is a neurological disorder, usually chronic, that causes an overwhelming urge to move one's legs. There is often an unpleasant feeling in the legs that improves temporarily by moving them. This feeling is often described as aching, tingling, or crawling in nature. Occasionally, arms may also be affected. The feelings generally happen when at rest and therefore can make it hard to sleep. Sleep disruption may leave people with RLS sleepy during the day, with low energy, and irritable or depressed. Additionally, many have limb twitching during sleep, a condition known as periodic limb movement disorder (PLMD). RLS is not the same as habitual foot-tapping or leg-rocking.

Scabies is a contagious human skin infestation by the tiny (0.2–0.45 mm) mite Sarcoptes scabiei, variety hominis. It is a particular public health problem in crowded settings such as care homes, schools, refugee camps, prisons, and hospitals. The most common symptoms are severe itchiness and a pimple-like rash. Occasionally, tiny burrows may appear on the skin. In a first-ever infection, symptoms usually develop within two to six weeks. During reinfection, symptoms may begin within 24 hours. The itch is often worse at night.
precaution 1: Apply prescribed permethrin cream to entire body
precaution 2: Wash clothes and bedding in hot water

description: Scarlet fever, also known as scarlatina and scarlatiniform rash, is an infectious disease caused by Streptococcus pyogenes, a Group A streptococcus (GAS). It most commonly affects children and young adolescents between five and 15 years of age. The signs and symptoms include a sore throat, fever, headache, swollen lymph nodes, and a characteristic rash.
precaution 1: Complete full course of prescribed antibiotics
precaution 2: Isolate until 24 hours after starting antibiotics

description: Schizophrenia is a mental disorder characterized variously by hallucinations, delusions, disorganized thinking or behavior, and flat or inappropriate affect. Symptoms develop gradually and typically begin during young adulthood and rarely resolve. There is no objective diagnostic test; diagnosis is based on observed behavior, a psychiatric history, and reports of others familiar with the person.

Sinusitis, also known as rhinosinusitis and commonly known as a sinus infection, is an inflammation of the mucous membranes that line the sinuses resulting in symptoms that may include production of thick nasal mucus, nasal congestion, facial congestion, facial pain, facial pressure, loss of smell, or fever.

Streptococcal pharyngitis, also known as streptococcal sore throat, is pharyngitis caused by Streptococcus pyogenes, a gram-positive, group A streptococcus. Common symptoms include fever, sore throat, red tonsils, and enlarged lymph nodes in the front of the neck.

Stress incontinence, also known as stress urinary incontinence (SUI) or effort incontinence is a form of urinary incontinence. It is due to inadequate closure of the bladder outlet by the urethral sphincter.

Syphilis is a sexually transmitted infection caused by the bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum. The signs and symptoms depend on the stage it presents: primary, secondary, latent or tertiary.
precaution 3: Complete full course of penicillin treatment

description: Rosacea is a long-term skin condition that typically affects the face. It results in redness, pimples, swelling, and small and superficial dilated blood vessels.

Temporomandibular joint dysfunction is an umbrella term for disorders causing pain and dysfunction of the muscles of mastication and TMJ. Common symptoms include jaw pain, restricted movement, and clicking sounds. Though not life-threatening, chronic pain may impair quality of life.
Tension-type headache (TTH) is the most common primary headache. Pain typically radiates from neck or scalp muscles and affects both sides of the head. It accounts for nearly 90% of headaches.
Tendinopathy is a tendon disorder causing pain, swelling, and impaired movement, often worse with activity. Commonly affects shoulder, elbow, wrist, hip, knee, or ankle.
Lateral epicondylitis is an enthesopathy of the extensor carpi radialis brevis origin causing lateral elbow pain and tenderness. Pain may radiate into the forearm.
Tinnitus is perception of ringing or noise without external sound stimulus. It may be intermittent or persistent.
Tonsillitis is inflammation of the tonsils causing sore throat, fever, difficulty swallowing, and enlarged lymph nodes. Acute cases often viral or bacterial.
Trichomoniasis is a sexually transmitted infection caused by Trichomonas vaginalis. Women are often symptomatic with discharge and irritation; men are frequently asymptomatic.
UTI is infection of urinary tract affecting bladder (cystitis) or kidney (pyelonephritis). Symptoms include dysuria, urgency, lower abdominal pain, and sometimes fever.
Vaginal yeast infection (candidal vulvovaginitis) is overgrowth of yeast causing itching, burning, and thick white discharge.
Vitamin B12 deficiency is low serum B12 levels leading to anemia and possible neurological symptoms. Risk higher in elderly and vegetarians.
Vitamin D deficiency impairs bone mineralization causing rickets in children and osteomalacia in adults. Often due to low sun exposure.
Whooping cough is a highly contagious bacterial respiratory disease with prolonged severe coughing fits lasting weeks.
